sections.caption: Marsa is financial planning software for wealth advisors. Here's one session in full.
Omar is 42. Senior manager at a multinational. Wife, two kids, seven and ten. Around 155,000 pounds come in each month. 3 million already invested, 40,000 saved on top.
Three goals. An apartment in New Cairo by 2028. University for the kids in 2033. Retirement at 60.
But what Omar wants isn't just "which university." It's a whole lifestyle. So the advisor runs three scenarios side by side. Each one a different life path.
Scenario one. "Everything I want." AUC for both kids. Retirement at 60 sustaining today's lifestyle. Target: 55 million. Marsa: out of reach.
Scenario two. Middle path. GUC or BUE. Retirement at 60, slightly leaner. Target: 33 million. Marsa: aspirational. The median just clears.
Scenario three. Pragmatic. Cairo University. Retirement pushed to 63. Target: 15 million. Marsa: attainable. The pessimistic path clears.
Marsa's inversion tells the advisor what changes the verdict. The monthly savings needed. Or the retirement date that closes the gap.
Three paths. Three honest verdicts. One conversation about the trade-off.
Marsa. Financial planning, built for the real conversation.
